behavior_summary: This script implements a guard-enforcement sequence: when invoked it checks limits and NPC state, accuses a target, sequences spoken barks, and applies punishments/effects such as stasis, spawn effects, temporary items, fire-spell visuals, and teleportation.

It defers or aborts when global limits are reached (a pre-check compared to 0x14) or when the relevant guard NPC is dead, and it attempts to validate candidate positions before placing effects or teleporting the accused.
Speech is synchronized (inferred somebodyTalking behavior) and the routine performs cleanup of temporary items and clears combat state at the end.
notable_control_flow: Early abort if FREE::1E6A pre-check indicates the routine should not proceed (return compared against 0x14).
Abort/skip if the guard NPC is dead (Npc::isDead check).
Branching on an entry flag parameter ([BP+0Ah]) to select different accusation/sentencing lines and associated effect sequences.
Sequence of actions gated by validation checks (canExistAt-style checks via coordinate computations and FREE spawn helpers) before creating/popping temporary items or teleporting the target.
Sets/updates a global guard-call indicator (guardsCalled) and uses short waits/timers (FREE::2A56, FREE::1C19, METHOD::0113) to pace barks and effects.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning and naming of the numeric pre-check (FREE::1E6A) and the reason for the threshold 0x14 (20) — inferred as a guard-call rate/limit but not proven.
Precise identity and semantics of the entry_flag byte at [BP+0Ah] (which accusation modes it encodes and whether it maps to in-world crime types).

The exact effect and mechanics of several spawned methods (METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7, BODYPART::0080, FIRESPEL::1F65) — their purposes are inferred from call patterns but visuals/damage behavior are not confirmed.
Whether 'pid' always refers to the player avatar or may sometimes be another NPC/actor in context (inferred from repeated use but not explicitly typed).

main_function.name: U8P_CALGUARD::0080
main_function.purpose: Handle a guard NPC's reaction to an observed crime/incident: validate whether enforcement should occur, produce accusation/sentencing barks, spawn visual/effect sequences (stasis/fire/animations/items), possibly teleport or punish the accused, and clean up state.
entry_conditions: Invoked when a guard should process an offending party or an area-triggered crime event (inferred from repeated NPC/item calls and use of an entry_flag).
Routine checks a precondition (FREE::1E6A) and the guard's alive state before proceeding.

function_map.name: U8P_CALGUARD::0080
function_map.role: Guard enforcement handler (accuse, sentence, spawn effects, teleport and cleanup).

function_map.name: FREE::1E6A
function_map.role: Pre-check/count helper used to rate-limit or gate guard actions.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Check whether a given NPC is dead (used to abort behavior).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short spoken/printed line from an actor/item (accusations and replies).

function_map.name: U8P_CALGUARD::setAvatarInStasis
function_map.role: Place the avatar/target into stasis as part of sentencing.

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Read X coordinate from context item/NPC (used to compute spawn/teleport positions).

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Read Y coordinate from context item/NPC.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Read Z coordinate from context item/NPC.

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: Create a temporary item used for visual/effect placement.

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Place/pop the created temporary item into the world at chosen coordinates.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Destroy temporary items to clean up after effects.

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Teleport the accused to a computed destination as part of punishment.

function_map.name: Npc::clrInCombat
function_map.role: Clear combat state for the NPC after enforcement concludes.

external_calls.purpose: Rate-limit/pre-check to decide whether guard logic should proceed (return is compared to 0x14).
Verify the guard or context NPC is alive; abort if dead.
Make the guard and/or accused speak accusation and sentencing lines; used multiple times for speech sequencing.
Apply stasis punishment to the avatar/target for a duration during sentencing.
Compute facing/direction from guard/context to target for orienting spawned effects.
Spawn a short directional animation/action oriented at the accused (used repeatedly with directions and actor id).
Spawned visual/effect helper used for directional effects around accused or guard.
Small wait/timing or bookkeeping call to pace barks and actions.
Spawned routine to create ground/projectile/visual effect at computed coordinates (used for fire and similar effects).
Spawned fire-spell visual/damage effect used after a 'consign you to the flames' line during punishment.
Create temporary items used as part of visual/spawn effects before popping them into the world.
Place a created temporary item into the world at validated coordinates to realize an effect.
Destroy temporary items used for effects during cleanup.
Teleport the accused as part of punishment; coordinates and map are computed/validated first.
Clear the guard's or target's in-combat flag at the end of the sequence.
Spawned helper to generate a short reply/animation from the accused (e.g., 'I can explain!').
Small timing/UX helper invoked repeatedly for pacing or minor visual cues during the punishment sequence.

reads.name: guardsCalled
reads.likely_effect: Used to decide whether to proceed with guard enforcement (rate-limiting or avoiding repeated simultaneous guard actions).

writes.name: guardsCalled
writes.likely_effect: Set/updated to record that a guard action has been initiated so subsequent calls can be gated.

writes.name: somebodyTalking
writes.likely_effect: Set during speech sequences to prevent overlapping dialogue from other processes (inferred from sequencing/suspend behavior).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a guard-enforcement sequence: when invoked it checks limits and NPC state, accuses a target, sequences spoken barks, and applies punishments/effects such as stasis, spawn effects, temporary items, fire-spell visuals, and teleportation.
